name: municipal_services_communications_outage_cedar_jus_mer
premise: A Municipal Services response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving municipal services communications outage cedar jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Ansel Haddad
agents.identity: I am Ansel Haddad, accountable for a defensible call in Municipal Services when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Sana Sato
agents.identity: I am Sana Sato, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Municipal Services.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Ansel Haddad must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Ansel Haddad do?
